payload.objective: Review the generated inbox runner together with its reviewed delegate script from the local inbox pipeline and provide a structured verdict using one of: pass, fail, needs_revision. Always output a review markdown artifact and include verdict in metadata.

params.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)
params.description:
Purpose:

  Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team.

‌

  Expected behavior:

- read-only Trello ingest

- preview creation smoke only

- no business execution claim

- no Trello writeback expected in this step

‌

  Traceability:

- backlog: BL-20260324-014

- blocker context: BL-20260324-015

- created_by: Oscarling

- created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai

‌

  Note:

  This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished.

Execution contract: treat this as a best-effort, evidence-backed PDF extraction/conversion attempt. Do not claim OCR success without evidence. If full OCR/Excel conversion is not honestly achievable, return reviewable intermediate artifacts, explicit limitations, and next-step guidance. Keep behavior deterministic and limited to declared local artifacts.

review_scope.goal: Audit the wrapper and the reviewed delegate together so the review evidence can speak to the end-to-end readonly smoke path.

artifact_snapshots.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Readonly Trello PDF→Excel inbox runner.

This wrapper keeps automation reviewable by delegating to the reviewed
artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py script, capturing its evidence,
and enforcing success gates that align with repository policy.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

DEFAULT_TITLE = "BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)"
DEFAULT_DESCRIPTION = (
    "Purpose: | Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team. "
    "| Expected behavior: | - read-only Trello ingest | - preview creation smoke only "
    "| - no business execution claim | - no Trello writeback expected in this step "
    "| Traceability: | - backlog: BL-20260324-014 | - blocker context: BL-20260324-015 "
    "| - created_by: Oscarling | - created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai "
    "| Note: | This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished."
)
DEFAULT_LABELS = ["best_effort", "evidence_backed", "readonly", "reviewable", "trello"]
DEFAULT_ORIGIN_ID = "trello:69c24cd3c1a2359ddd7a1bf8"
DEFAULT_DELEGATE_SCRIPT = "artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py"

STATUS_EXIT_CODE = {"success": 0, "partial": 2, "failed": 1}


def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Run the reviewed PDF→Excel OCR delegate with Trello smoke metadata."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--input-dir",
        default="~/Desktop/pdf样本",
        help="Directory containing PDF samples (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output-xlsx",
        default="artifacts/outputs/trello_readonly/pdf_to_excel_from_trello.xlsx",
        help="Path for the generated XLSX workbook (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--ocr-mode",
        default="auto",
        choices=["auto", "on", "off"],
        help="OCR mode passed to the delegate (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        dest="dry_run",
        action="store_true",
        help="Run the delegate in dry-run/preview mode.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--no-dry-run",
        dest="dry_run",
        action="store_false",
        help="Force non-dry-run execution (default).",
    )
    parser.set_defaults(dry_run=False)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--timeout",
        type=int,
        default=900,
        help="Timeout window (seconds) applied to the delegate invocation (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--delegate-script",
        default=DEFAULT_DELEGATE_SCRIPT,
        help="Path to the reviewed pdf_to_excel_ocr.py script (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--origin-id",
        default=DEFAULT_ORIGIN_ID,
        help="Traceability origin identifier (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--title",
        default=DEFAULT_TITLE,
        help="Run title metadata (default: %(default)s).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        default=DEFAULT_DESCRIPTION,
        help="Run description metadata describing purpose/traceability.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--labels",
        nargs="*",
        default=list(DEFAULT_LABELS),
        help="Label metadata for downstream traceability.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--persist-report-json",
        dest="persist_report_json",
        help="Optional path to persist the delegate report for later inspection.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--extra-delegate-arg",
        dest="extra_delegate_args",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Additional raw arguments forwarded verbatim to the delegate (repeatable).",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def resolve_delegate_path(delegate_script: str, script_dir: Path) -> Path:
    delegate_path = Path(delegate_script)
    if not delegate_path.is_absolute():
        repo_root = script_dir.parent.parent
        candidate = (repo_root / delegate_path).resolve()
        if candidate.is_file():
            return candidate
        candidate = (script_dir / Path(delegate_script).name).resolve()
        if candidate.is_file():
            return candidate
    elif delegate_path.is_file():
        return delegate_path
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"Delegate script not found at {delegate_script}")


def build_delegate_command(
    delegate_path: Path,
    input_dir: Path,
    output_xlsx: Path,
    args: argparse.Namespace,
    report_path: Path,
) -> List[str]:
    command: List[str] = [
        sys.executable,
        str(delegate_path),
        "--input-dir",
        str(input_dir),
        "--output-xlsx",
        str(output_xlsx),
        "--ocr-mode",
        args.ocr_mode,
        "--report-json",
        str(report_path),
    ]
    if args.dry_run:
        command.append("--dry-run")
    if args.extra_delegate_args:
        command.extend(args.extra_delegate_args)
    return command


def try_parse_json_from_text(text: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    stripped = text.strip()
    if not stripped:
        return None
    candidates = [stripped]
    lines = [line.strip() for line in stripped.splitlines() if line.strip()]
    candidates.extend(reversed(lines))
    first = stripped.find("{")
    last = stripped.rfind("}")
    if first != -1 and last != -1 and last > first:
        candidates.append(stripped[first : last + 1])
    for candidate in candidates:
        if not candidate.startswith("{"):
            continue
        try:
            return json.loads(candidate)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            continue
    return None


def run_delegate_process(
    command: List[str], report_path: Path, timeout: int
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    start_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    start_monotonic = time.monotonic()
    timed_out = False
    stdout_text = ""
    stderr_text = ""
    exit_code: Optional[int] = None
    sidecar_error: Optional[str] = None
    try:
        completed = subprocess.run(
            command,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=timeout,
            check=False,
        )
        stdout_text = completed.stdout or ""
        stderr_text = completed.stderr or ""
        exit_code = completed.returncode
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
        timed_out = True
        stdout_text = exc.stdout or ""
        timeout_note = f"\n[automation] Delegate timed out after {timeout} seconds."
        stderr_text = (exc.stderr or "") + timeout_note
    duration = time.monotonic() - start_monotonic
    end_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    report_data: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    report_source: Optional[str] = None
    if not timed_out and report_path.exists():
        try:
            report_data = json.loads(report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            report_source = "sidecar"
        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
            sidecar_error = f"Failed to parse sidecar JSON: {exc}"
    if report_data is None and stdout_text.strip():
        fallback = try_parse_json_from_text(stdout_text)
        if fallback is not None:
            report_data = fallback
            report_source = "stdout"

    return {
        "command": command,
        "exit_code": exit_code,
        "timed_out": timed_out,
        "stdout": stdout_text,
        "stderr": stderr_text,
        "duration_seconds": duration,
        "started_at": start_time.isoformat(),
        "ended_at": end_time.isoformat(),
        "report_data": report_data,
        "report_source": report_source,
        "sidecar_error": sidecar_error,
        "report_path": str(report_path),
    }


def ensure_xlsx_extension(output_path: Path) -> None:
    if output_path.suffix.lower() != ".xlsx":
        raise ValueError(
            f"Output path must end with .xlsx (received: {output_path.name})"
        )


def text_excerpt(payload: str, limit: int = 2000) -> str:
    payload = payload.strip()
    if len(payload) <= limit:
        return payload
    return f"{payload[:limit]}… (truncated, total {len(payload)} chars)"


def persist_report_copy(report_data: Dict[str, Any], destination: Path) -> None:
    destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    destination.write_text(json.dumps(report_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")


def evaluate_delegate_outcome(
    delegate_run: Dict[str, Any],
    args: argparse.Namespace,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    delegate_report = delegate_run.get("report_data")
    limitations: List[str] = []
    next_steps: List[str] = []
    reason: str
    status: str

    if delegate_run["timed_out"]:
        status = "failed"
        reason = f"Delegate execution exceeded the {args.timeout}s timeout window."
        limitations.append(reason)
        next_steps.append("Re-run with a larger --timeout or investigate delegate performance bottlenecks.")
        return {
            "status": status,
            "reason": reason,
            "limitations": limitations,
            "next_steps": next_steps,
        }

    if delegate_report is None:
        status = "failed"
        reason = "Delegate report was not produced; cannot verify workbook evidence."
        limitations.append(reason)
        if delegate_run.get("sidecar_error"):
            limitations.append(delegate_run["sidecar_error"])
        next_steps.append("Inspect delegate stdout/stderr for JSON parsing issues and rerun after fixing the root cause.")
        return {
            "status": status,
            "reason": reason,
            "limitations": limitations,
            "next_steps": next_steps,
        }

    delegate_status = str(delegate_report.get("status") or "").lower()
    status_counter = delegate_report.get("status_counter") or {}
    total_files = delegate_report.get("total_files")
    dry_run_reported = bool(delegate_report.get("dry_run"))
    ocr_runtime_status = str(delegate_report.get("ocr_runtime_status") or "").lower()
    gates_failed: List[str] = []

    if delegate_status == "success":
        if args.dry_run or dry_run_reported:
            gates_failed.append("Readonly dry-run mode cannot claim full success for workbook output.")
        if not isinstance(total_files, int) or total_files < 1:
            gates_failed.append("Delegate did not confirm processing at least one PDF artifact.")
        failed_count = status_counter.get("failed", 0)
        partial_count = status_counter.get("partial", 0)
        if failed_count:
            gates_failed.append(f"Delegate reported failed files: {failed_count}")
        if partial_count:
            gates_failed.append(f"Delegate reported partial files: {partial_count}")
        if not delegate_report.get("excel_written"):
            gates_failed.append("Delegate did not confirm writing Excel workbook content.")
        if not delegate_report.get("output_exists"):
            gates_failed.append("Delegate could not attest that the XLSX output path exists.")
        output_size = delegate_report.get("output_size_bytes")
        if not isinstance(output_size, (int, float)) or output_size <= 0:
            gates_failed.append("Delegate did not report a positive XLSX byte size.")
        if args.ocr_mode in {"auto", "on"} and ocr_runtime_status in {"blocked", "partial"}:
            gates_failed.append(
                f"OCR runtime status reported as {ocr_runtime_status}; not enough evidence for full success."
            )
        if delegate_run.get("exit_code") not in (0, None):
            gates_failed.append(f"Delegate exited with non-zero status {delegate_run['exit_code']}.")

        if gates_failed:
            status = "partial"
            reason = "Delegate signaled success but repository success gates were not fully satisfied."
            limitations.extend(gates_failed)
            next_steps.append("Review delegate evidence and rerun once gating gaps are resolved.")
        else:
            status = "success"
            reason = "Delegate conversion succeeded with evidence-backed XLSX output."
    elif delegate_status == "partial":
        status = "partial"
        reason = delegate_report.get("message") or "Delegate reported partial status."
        limitations.append(reason)
        if isinstance(delegate_report.get("limitations"), list):
            limitations.extend(str(item) for item in delegate_report["limitations"])
        if not limitations:
            limitations.append("Delegate reported partial outcome without additional detail.")
        next_steps.append("Inspect delegate per-file details and address the blocked PDFs before rerunning.")
    else:
        status = "failed"
        if delegate_status:
            reason = f"Delegate reported failure status '{delegate_status}'."
        else:
            reason = "Delegate did not provide a recognizable status."
        limitations.append(reason)
        next_steps.append("Inspect delegate stderr/traceback to resolve the failure before rerunning.")

    if status == "partial" and not next_steps:
        next_steps.append("Gather missing evidence or address noted limitations, then rerun the smoke test.")

    return {
        "status": status,
        "reason": reason,
        "limitations": limitations,
        "next_steps": next_steps,
    }


def build_summary(
    args: argparse.Namespace,
    input_dir: Path,
    output_xlsx: Path,
    delegate_path: Path,
    delegate_run: Dict[str, Any],
    evaluation: Dict[str, Any],
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    summary: Dict[str, Any] = {
        "status": evaluation["status"],
        "reason": evaluation["reason"],
        "title": args.title,
        "origin_id": args.origin_id,
        "description": args.description,
        "labels": args.labels,
        "readonly_semantics": "Read-only Trello preview; local filesystem writes only when dry_run=False.",
        "input": {
            "input_dir": str(input_dir),
            "ocr_mode": args.ocr_mode,
            "dry_run": args.dry_run,
        },
        "output": {
            "output_xlsx": str(output_xlsx),
        },
        "delegate": {
            "script": str(delegate_path),
            "command": delegate_run["command"],
            "exit_code": delegate_run["exit_code"],
            "timed_out": delegate_run["timed_out"],
            "report_source": delegate_run.get("report_source"),
            "report_path": delegate_run.get("report_path"),
            "sidecar_error": delegate_run.get("sidecar_error"),
            "report": delegate_run.get("report_data"),
            "stdout_excerpt": text_excerpt(delegate_run.get("stdout", "")),
            "stderr_excerpt": text_excerpt(delegate_run.get("stderr", "")),
            "started_at": delegate_run.get("started_at"),
            "ended_at": delegate_run.get("ended_at"),
            "duration_seconds": delegate_run.get("duration_seconds"),
        },
        "limitations": evaluation["limitations"],
        "next_steps": evaluation["next_steps"],
    }
    return summary


def run_workflow(args: argparse.Namespace) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    script_dir = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    delegate_path = resolve_delegate_path(args.delegate_script, script_dir)

    input_dir = Path(args.input_dir).expanduser()
    output_xlsx = Path(args.output_xlsx).expanduser()
    ensure_xlsx_extension(output_xlsx)
    output_xlsx.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="pdf_to_excel_runner_") as tmpdir:
        report_path = Path(tmpdir) / "delegate_report.json"
        command = build_delegate_command(delegate_path, input_dir, output_xlsx, args, report_path)
        delegate_run = run_delegate_process(command, report_path, args.timeout)

        report_data = delegate_run.get("report_data")
        if args.persist_report_json and report_data is not None:
            persist_report_copy(report_data, Path(args.persist_report_json).expanduser())

        evaluation = evaluate_delegate_outcome(delegate_run, args)
        return build_summary(args, input_dir, output_xlsx, delegate_path, delegate_run, evaluation)


def main() -> None:
    args = parse_args()
    try:
        summary = run_workflow(args)
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        summary = {
            "status": "failed",
            "reason": "Wrapper exception raised before delegate completion.",
            "title": args.title,
            "origin_id": args.origin_id,
            "description": args.description,
            "labels": args.labels,
            "readonly_semantics": "Read-only Trello preview; local filesystem writes only when dry_run=False.",
            "input": {
                "input_dir": args.input_dir,
                "ocr_mode": args.ocr_mode,
                "dry_run": args.dry_run,
            },
            "output": {
                "output_xlsx": args.output_xlsx,
            },
            "delegate": {
                "script": args.delegate_script,
                "command": None,
                "exit_code": None,
                "timed_out": False,
                "report_source": None,
                "report_path": None,
                "sidecar_error": None,
                "report": None,
                "stdout_excerpt": "",
                "stderr_excerpt": "",
                "started_at": None,
                "ended_at": None,
                "duration_seconds": None,
            },
            "limitations": [f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"],
            "next_steps": ["Review the stack trace, fix the wrapper issue, and rerun the smoke conversion."],
        }

    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
    exit_code = STATUS_EXIT_CODE.get(summary["status"], 1)
    sys.exit(exit_code)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Batch PDF -> Excel extractor with optional OCR fallback.

Design goals:
- Batch processing with per-file isolation (single file failure must not stop the batch)
- OCR mode: auto | on | off
- Chinese-friendly OCR defaults (chi_sim+eng)
- Dry-run support for pilot validation when dependencies are unavailable
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import shutil
import sys
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any


try:
    from pypdf import PdfReader  # type: ignore
except Exception:
    PdfReader = None

try:
    import pandas as pd  # type: ignore
except Exception:
    pd = None

try:
    import pytesseract  # type: ignore
except Exception:
    pytesseract = None

try:
    from pdf2image import convert_from_path  # type: ignore
except Exception:
    convert_from_path = None


@dataclass
class FileResult:
    file_name: str
    file_path: str
    status: str
    extract_method: str
    page_count: int
    text_chars: int
    text_preview: str
    warnings: str
    error: str


def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Batch PDF to Excel extractor with OCR fallback.")
    parser.add_argument("--input-dir", required=True, help="Directory containing PDF files.")
    parser.add_argument("--output-xlsx", required=True, help="Output Excel path.")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--ocr",
        choices=("auto", "on", "off"),
        default="auto",
        help="OCR mode. auto=use OCR when plain text extraction is weak.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="Do not write Excel file. Print execution summary only.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--ocr-lang",
        default="chi_sim+eng",
        help="OCR language pack for pytesseract, default supports Chinese + English.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--auto-ocr-min-chars",
        type=int,
        default=50,
        help="In auto mode, run OCR when extracted text chars < this threshold.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--report-json",
        default="",
        help="Optional sidecar path for writing the same JSON report emitted to stdout.",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def discover_pdfs(input_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:
    if not input_dir.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Input directory does not exist: {input_dir}")
    if not input_dir.is_dir():
        raise NotADirectoryError(f"Input path is not a directory: {input_dir}")
    files = sorted([p for p in input_dir.rglob("*") if p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() == ".pdf"])
    return files


def detect_ocr_runtime_status() -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    missing: list[str] = []
    present: list[str] = []

    if pytesseract is None:
        missing.append("python module pytesseract")
    else:
        present.append("python module pytesseract")
    if convert_from_path is None:
        missing.append("python module pdf2image")
    else:
        present.append("python module pdf2image")
    if shutil.which("tesseract") is None:
        missing.append("binary tesseract")
    else:
        present.append("binary tesseract")
    if shutil.which("pdftoppm") is None:
        missing.append("binary pdftoppm (poppler)")
    else:
        present.append("binary pdftoppm (poppler)")

    if not missing:
        return "available", []
    if present:
        return "partial", missing
    return "blocked", missing


def extract_text_pypdf(pdf_path: Path) -> tuple[str, int]:
    if PdfReader is None:
        raise RuntimeError("pypdf not installed")
    reader = PdfReader(str(pdf_path))
    page_texts: list[str] = []
    for page in reader.pages:
        text = page.extract_text() or ""
        page_texts.append(text)
    return "\n".join(page_texts), len(reader.pages)


def extract_text_ocr(pdf_path: Path, ocr_lang: str) -> str:
    if pytesseract is None:
        raise RuntimeError("pytesseract not installed")
    if convert_from_path is None:
        raise RuntimeError("pdf2image not installed")
    if shutil.which("tesseract") is None:
        raise RuntimeError("tesseract binary not found")
    if shutil.which("pdftoppm") is None:
        raise RuntimeError("pdftoppm binary not found (install poppler)")

    images = convert_from_path(str(pdf_path), dpi=220)
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for image in images:
        chunks.append(pytesseract.image_to_string(image, lang=ocr_lang))
    return "\n".join(chunks)


def compact_preview(text: str, limit: int = 160) -> str:
    single_line = " ".join(text.split())
    if len(single_line) <= limit:
        return single_line
    return single_line[: limit - 3] + "..."


def process_one_pdf(
    pdf_path: Path,
    ocr_mode: str,
    ocr_lang: str,
    auto_ocr_min_chars: int,
) -> FileResult:
    warnings: list[str] = []
    errors: list[str] = []
    method = "none"
    text = ""
    page_count = 0

    try:
        text, page_count = extract_text_pypdf(pdf_path)
        method = "text"
    except Exception as e:
        warnings.append(f"text extraction unavailable: {e}")

    needs_ocr = False
    if ocr_mode == "on":
        needs_ocr = True
    elif ocr_mode == "auto" and len(text.strip()) < auto_ocr_min_chars:
        needs_ocr = True

    if needs_ocr:
        try:
            ocr_text = extract_text_ocr(pdf_path, ocr_lang).strip()
            if ocr_text:
                if text.strip():
                    text = f"{text.strip()}\n\n[OCR Fallback]\n{ocr_text}"
                    method = "text+ocr"
                else:
                    text = ocr_text
                    method = "ocr"
            else:
                warnings.append("OCR returned empty text")
        except Exception as e:
            errors.append(f"OCR failed: {e}")

    if not text.strip():
        if errors:
            status = "failed"
        else:
            status = "partial"
            warnings.append("No extractable text captured")
    else:
        if errors:
            status = "partial"
            warnings.append("Text was extracted, but one or more extraction attempts failed; review errors")
        else:
            status = "success"

    return FileResult(
        file_name=pdf_path.name,
        file_path=str(pdf_path),
        status=status,
        extract_method=method,
        page_count=page_count,
        text_chars=len(text),
        text_preview=compact_preview(text),
        warnings=" | ".join(warnings),
        error=" | ".join(errors),
    )


def write_excel(results: list[FileResult], output_xlsx: Path) -> None:
    if pd is None:
        raise RuntimeError("pandas is required to write Excel output")
    output_xlsx.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    rows = [asdict(r) for r in results]
    detail_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)

    summary_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    by_status = detail_df.groupby("status").size().to_dict()
    by_method = detail_df.groupby("extract_method").size().to_dict()
    for key, value in sorted(by_status.items()):
        summary_rows.append({"metric": "status_count", "name": key, "value": int(value)})
    for key, value in sorted(by_method.items()):
        summary_rows.append({"metric": "method_count", "name": key, "value": int(value)})
    summary_rows.append({"metric": "total_files", "name": "all", "value": int(len(results))})
    summary_df = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows)

    with pd.ExcelWriter(output_xlsx) as writer:
        summary_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="summary", index=False)
        detail_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="files", index=False)


def emit_report(report: dict[str, Any], report_json: str) -> None:
    rendered = json.dumps(report, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    print(rendered)
    if not report_json:
        return
    out_path = Path(report_json).expanduser().resolve()
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out_path.write_text(rendered + "\n", encoding="utf-8")


def build_report_template(
    *,
    input_dir: Path,
    output_xlsx: Path,
    ocr_mode: str,
    dry_run: bool,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    return {
        "status": "failed",
        "input_dir": str(input_dir),
        "output_xlsx": str(output_xlsx),
        "ocr_mode": ocr_mode,
        "ocr_runtime_status": "unknown",
        "ocr_missing_dependencies": [],
        "total_files": 0,
        "files": [],
        "status_counter": {},
        "dry_run": bool(dry_run),
        "extraction_status": "none",
        "export_status": "not_started",
        "excel_written": False,
        "output_exists": False,
        "output_size_bytes": 0,
        "notes": [],
        "next_steps": [],
        "error": "",
    }


def main() -> int:
    args = parse_args()
    input_dir = Path(args.input_dir).expanduser().resolve()
    output_xlsx = Path(args.output_xlsx).expanduser().resolve()

    try:
        pdf_files = discover_pdfs(input_dir)
    except Exception as e:
        report = build_report_template(
            input_dir=input_dir,
            output_xlsx=output_xlsx,
            ocr_mode=args.ocr,
            dry_run=args.dry_run,
        )
        report["error"] = str(e)
        report["extraction_status"] = "none"
        report["export_status"] = "not_started"
        report["notes"].append("Input discovery failed before extraction could start.")
        report["next_steps"].append("Verify input directory exists and is readable, then rerun.")
        emit_report(report, args.report_json)
        return 2

    if not pdf_files:
        report = build_report_template(
            input_dir=input_dir,
            output_xlsx=output_xlsx,
            ocr_mode=args.ocr,
            dry_run=args.dry_run,
        )
        report["status"] = "partial"
        report["extraction_status"] = "none"
        report["export_status"] = "skipped_no_input"
        report["notes"] = [f"No PDF files found under {input_dir}"]
        report["next_steps"] = [
            "Add one or more .pdf files under the input directory and rerun.",
        ]
        emit_report(report, args.report_json)
        return 0

    ocr_runtime, missing = detect_ocr_runtime_status()
    results: list[FileResult] = []
    for pdf in pdf_files:
        try:
            results.append(
                process_one_pdf(
                    pdf,
                    ocr_mode=args.ocr,
                    ocr_lang=args.ocr_lang,
                    auto_ocr_min_chars=args.auto_ocr_min_chars,
                )
            )
        except Exception as e:
            results.append(
                FileResult(
                    file_name=pdf.name,
                    file_path=str(pdf),
                    status="failed",
                    extract_method="none",
                    page_count=0,
                    text_chars=0,
                    text_preview="",
                    warnings="",
                    error=f"Unhandled processing exception: {e}",
                )
            )

    status_counter: dict[str, int] = {}
    for item in results:
        status_counter[item.status] = status_counter.get(item.status, 0) + 1

    files_payload = [asdict(item) for item in results]
    failed_count = status_counter.get("failed", 0)
    partial_count = status_counter.get("partial", 0)
    if failed_count == 0 and partial_count == 0:
        extraction_status = "success"
    else:
        extraction_status = "partial"

    notes: list[str] = []
    next_steps: list[str] = []
    if ocr_runtime != "available":
        notes.append(f"OCR runtime status is {ocr_runtime}; missing dependencies: {', '.join(missing) or 'none'}")
        next_steps.append("Install missing OCR runtime dependencies if OCR fallback is required.")
    if partial_count > 0:
        notes.append(f"{partial_count} file(s) reported partial status.")
        next_steps.append("Inspect per-file partial records in `files` and address listed warnings/errors.")
    if failed_count > 0:
        notes.append(f"{failed_count} file(s) reported failed status.")
        next_steps.append("Inspect per-file failures in `files` and resolve the extraction or OCR error causes.")

    report = build_report_template(
        input_dir=input_dir,
        output_xlsx=output_xlsx,
        ocr_mode=args.ocr,
        dry_run=args.dry_run,
    )
    report.update(
        {
            "status": extraction_status,
            "ocr_runtime_status": ocr_runtime,
            "ocr_missing_dependencies": missing,
            "total_files": len(results),
            "files": files_payload,
            "status_counter": status_counter,
            "extraction_status": extraction_status,
            "export_status": "not_started",
            "notes": notes,
            "next_steps": next_steps,
        }
    )

    if args.dry_run:
        report["export_status"] = "skipped_dry_run"
        emit_report(report, args.report_json)
        return 0

    report["export_status"] = "running"
    try:
        write_excel(results, output_xlsx)
        report["output_exists"] = output_xlsx.exists() and output_xlsx.is_file()
        if report["output_exists"]:
            report["output_size_bytes"] = int(output_xlsx.stat().st_size)
        report["excel_written"] = bool(report["output_exists"] and report["output_size_bytes"] > 0)
        report["export_status"] = "succeeded" if report["excel_written"] else "failed"
        if report["export_status"] == "failed":
            report["status"] = "failed"
            report["notes"] = report.get("notes", []) + [
                "Excel write step completed without a usable XLSX artifact; treating export as failed."
            ]
            report["next_steps"] = report.get("next_steps", []) + [
                "Inspect output artifact path permissions and workbook writer dependencies.",
            ]
        emit_report(report, args.report_json)
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        report["status"] = "failed"
        report["error"] = str(e)
        report["output_exists"] = output_xlsx.exists() and output_xlsx.is_file()
        if report["output_exists"]:
            report["output_size_bytes"] = int(output_xlsx.stat().st_size)
        report["excel_written"] = bool(report["output_exists"] and report["output_size_bytes"] > 0)
        report["export_status"] = "failed"
        report["notes"] = report.get("notes", []) + [
            "Excel write step failed after extraction.",
            (
                f"Extraction phase completed with extraction_status={report.get('extraction_status', 'unknown')}; "
                "export_status=failed."
            ),
        ]
        report["next_steps"] = report.get("next_steps", []) + [
            "Inspect extraction evidence in `files`/`status_counter` even when export fails.",
            "Check pandas/openpyxl availability and output path permissions.",
        ]
        emit_report(report, args.report_json)
        return 3


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

review_contract.artifact_policy: Always include either `file_contents` for the review artifact path or `artifacts` referencing that exact path.
review_contract.fallback_policy: If evidence is insufficient, return `status=partial`, include explicit `errors`, still generate review artifact content, and set verdict=needs_revision.

constraints: Review must be grounded in produced automation artifact
When both wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are supplied, evaluate them as one end-to-end readonly pair instead of ignoring the delegate evidence.
Do not invent missing artifact content
Return a clear verdict: pass, fail, or needs_revision
Generate review artifact markdown for expected_outputs[0].path
Use artifact_snapshots when provided; avoid claiming access problems if snapshot content exists.
When both the generated wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are provided, review them together rather than silently narrowing scope to one file.
Return metadata.verdict with one of: pass, fail, needs_revision.
Always generate review artifact content for expected_outputs[0].path.
If evidence is insufficient, use partial + errors + verdict=needs_revision, but still output review artifact.

source.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)

acceptance_criteria: Produce a review artifact with explicit verdict (pass/fail/needs_revision)
